fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: North Carolina Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to North Carolina - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity north carolina, how to transfer occupational therapist license to north carolina, occupational therapist compact north carolina, north carolina occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: North Carolina Board of Occupational Therapy
board.mailing_address:
North Carolina Board of Occupational Therapy
PO Box 7006
Raleigh, NC 27628-7006

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination
quick_facts.ce_requirement: 30 contact hours of continuing competence activities per renewal cycle

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in North Carolina.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create Online Account
steps.description: Register and create an account on the NCBOT Online Licensing Portal (Thentia Cloud) to access the application forms.

steps.title: Complete Application and Pay Fee
steps.description: Fill out the appropriate online application (Licensure by Examination or Licensure by Endorsement) and pay the non-refundable application fee through the NCBOT Online Licensing Portal.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts, verifying graduation from an accredited occupational therapy program, to be sent directly from your academic institution to the North Carolina Board of Occupational Therapy.

steps.title: Verify NBCOT Certification and Scores
steps.description: Ensure your National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) certification status and examination scores are sent directly from NBCOT to the NCBOT.

steps.title: Fulfill Additional Requirements
steps.description: Provide any additional documentation requested by the Board, such as fieldwork verification or responses to background questions, through the NCBOT Online Licensing Portal or as directed.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBCOT exam. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

faqs.question: How much does a North Carolina OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around  to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does North Carolina have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: North Carolina has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to North Carolina?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in North Carolina while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

hero.title: Become a Occupational Therapist in North Carolina
hero.dek: Applicants for Occupational Therapist licensure in North Carolina must apply online through the NCBOT Online Licensing Portal (Thentia Cloud).
